nodes.label: SGR (Gradient Reconciliation for local learning)
nodes.rationale: A boundary-gradient matching penalty ‖delta_k - g_{k-1}‖^2 forces adjacent local blocks to agree on their shared boundary; >40% activation-memory saving, matches BP within ~1% by carrying one stored tensor per boundary.

nodes.label: Gradient-mismatch epsilon-floor
nodes.rationale: The per-boundary disagreement epsilon injects an additive error floor that stalls the final-block loss at a plateau; keeping ‖epsilon‖ small is the whole game. This is the economy-gate's WHY: a stall is real and worth detecting.
